ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF SANG KIL'S APPLICATION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DR. KIL HAS FAILED TO SHOW EITHER A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS OF HER SOLE CLAIM THAT DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY FAILED TO PROPERLY ENGAGE IN AN INTERACTIVE PROCESS OR THAT SHE WILL SUFFER HARM IF HER REQUEST FOR AN INJUNCTION IS DENIED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2018 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Csus Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY'S Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Off calendar. The case was dismissed on 10/05/18.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-09-2018 Set For Trial On Dec-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Continued To May-23-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Continued To Apr-18-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2017 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause (Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 8/17/17)
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Stephen F. Henry of the law offices of Stephen F. Henry (510-898-1883) appears as counsel for plaintiff/applicant party

Attorney, William C. Hsu, General Counsel of the Office of the Chancellor of the California State University (562-951-4500) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification:

Plaintiff Sang Kil's application for a preliminary injunction is denied without prejudice. Dr. Kil has failed to show either a likelihood of success on the merits of her sole claim that defendant Board of Trustees of the California State University failed to properly engage in an interactive process or that she will suffer harm if her request for an injunction is denied. Dr. Kil's evidentiary showing, which relies entirely on her very short and most conclusory verified complaint, is extremely weak given the nature of the matters at issue. Dr. Kil presents no evidence from any physician or other medical professional that it is necessary for her to teach, attend meetings and attend office hours remotely. CSU presented substantial evidence that it has engaged in the interactive process on numerous occasions as demonstrated by defendants' current Fifth Accommodation Letter. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
